meta.title: Eder Queiroz — Software Engineer
meta.description: Portfolio of Eder Queiroz: backend engineer (Node.js/NestJS, AWS, Flutter). Reliable, scalable systems — with live illustrated case studies.
hero.tagline: I build the systems that go invisible when they work: the notification that always arrives, the balance that's never redeemed twice, the queue that takes the load.
about.title: About
about.body: Full-stack developer at Grão Direto, where I enjoy working on the problems that go invisible when they succeed. Previously tech lead at bugWare — where I learned as much about architecture as about working with teams. I'm finishing a Computer Engineering degree at IFTM and keep one foot in research, from genetic algorithms to hackathons. What drives me is building software that solves real problems.
skills.title: Tools I actually use
skills.hint: Hover or focus each one to see where it went into battle.
skills.node.tip: Backbone of the backend at Grão Direto — services powering the whole platform.
skills.nest.tip: Framework of the services I maintain: well-organized modules, queues and transactions.
skills.aws.tip: Infrastructure of the systems I build — SQS, FIFO queues and DLQs in production.
skills.sqs.tip: The FIFO queue that killed Twilio rate-limit errors and took delivery to 100%.
skills.flutter.tip: Interactive trading timeline and dashboards, in production in Grão Direto's app.
skills.react.tip: Web platform front-end, including an A/B-tested signup journey (+12% conversion).
skills.postgres.tip: Where the points ledger lives — pessimistic locks and transactions guaranteeing consistency.
cases.title: Illustrated case studies
cases.subtitle: Three real problems I solved — animated so you can watch the idea work.
ledger.title: Loyalty: balance as a ledger
ledger.desc: Instead of a simple counter, I modeled the points balance as a ledger of expiring batches: FIFO consumption (oldest first) and full traceability. A database-level pessimistic lock eliminated duplicate redemptions under concurrency.
sqs.title: The queue that delivers 100%
sqs.desc: Twilio's API rate limit was dropping notifications. A FIFO queue (AWS SQS) serializes sends: transient failures go back to the queue, permanent failures are discarded, and messages that exhaust retries land in a DLQ with controlled redrive.
ga.title: Timetabling with a genetic algorithm
ga.desc: The project that earned me IFTM's honors student award: automatic timetable generation with genetic algorithms. This miniature evolves live until conflicts (a teacher repeated on the same day) reach zero.
wins.title: Achievements
wins.honor.desc: IFTM — timetabling project with genetic algorithms
exp.title: Experience
exp.gd.desc: Backend to mobile: loyalty system (ledger), SQS queues, facial validation and A/B tests that lifted signup conversion by 12%.
exp.bw.desc: Architecture and team leadership on custom projects.
exp.edu.desc: Research in optimization with genetic algorithms; hackathon wins on the record.
contact.title: Shall we talk?
contact.body: Backend, agtech, a hairy concurrency problem or just a good idea — drop me a line, it'll be a pleasure.
footer.built: Handmade with HTML, CSS and JS — no framework.
